White Matter Development: Analysis

Benjamin Risk

4/27/2022

library(mgcv)
## Loading required package: nlme
## This is mgcv 1.8-39. For overview type 'help("mgcv-package")'.
library(itsadug)
## Loading required package: plotfunctions
## Loaded package itsadug 2.4 (see 'help("itsadug")' ).
dat_harmonized = read.csv('dat_harmonized.csv',stringsAsFactors = TRUE)

Fx – Fornix (limbic, output of hippocampus)

IFOF – inferior-fronto-occipital-fasciculus (associative, connects occipital cortex to frontal lobe)

CCb – body of corpus callosum (commissural, connects hemispheres)

PT – pyramidal tract includes pyramidal neurons connecting motor neurons to brain stem or spinal cord (corticospinal and corticobulbar tracts)

FA

This function extracts the largest age at which there is a significant difference:

# note: full term is 37
maxChronAge = function(GAlow,GAhigh=40,model,ylab,less=TRUE,print.plot=FALSE) {
  a=plot_diff(model,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(GAlow,GAhigh)),sim.ci = TRUE, hide.label=TRUE,xlab='Chronological age (days)',ylab=ylab,plot=print.plot)
  if(less) {
  max(a$chor_age[a$est+a$sim.CI<0])
  } else {
    max(a$chor_age[a$est-a$sim.CI>0])
  }
}

# Fx and Pt can have opposite trend, become more dissimilar with age
minChronAge = function(GAlow,GAhigh=40,model,ylab,less=TRUE,print.plot=FALSE) {
  a=plot_diff(model,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(GAlow,GAhigh)),sim.ci = TRUE, hide.label=TRUE,xlab='Chronological age (days)',ylab=ylab,plot=print.plot)
  if(less) {
  min(a$chor_age[a$est+a$sim.CI<0])
  } else {
    min(a$chor_age[a$est-a$sim.CI>0])
  }
}

The syntax used here is described as the functional anova in the supporting R documentation for ?ti().

# Create plots at different gestational ages:
my.function.ga <- function(dti.tract, my.ylim = c(0.1,0.3), my.ylab,  my.main="", estMaxChronAge=TRUE,estMinChronAge=FALSE,print.legend=FALSE,less=TRUE) {
    model0 = gam(dti.tract~ti(chor_age,k=9)+ti(GA,k=5)+ti(chor_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+Sex+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
  # step into function to examine model diagnostics:
 # gam.check(model0)
  print(summary(model0))

  # vis.gam(model0,view=c('chor_age','GA'),theta=-45,ticktype='detailed',xlab='Chron Age',ylab='Gest Age',zlab='FA')
  set.seed(123)

  #tiff('../Figures/DiffCurves_FA.tiff',width=720,height=480,pointsize=20)
  if (estMaxChronAge) {
      # par(mfrow=c(2,3))
      (ga.35=maxChronAge(GAlow=35,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.36=maxChronAge(GAlow=36,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.37=maxChronAge(GAlow=37,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.38=maxChronAge(GAlow=38,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.39=maxChronAge(GAlow=39,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.41=maxChronAge(GAlow=40,GAhigh=41,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      #dev.off()
  }
  
  if (estMinChronAge) {
      # par(mfrow=c(2,3))
      (ga.35=minChronAge(GAlow=35,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.36=minChronAge(GAlow=36,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.37=minChronAge(GAlow=37,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.38=minChronAge(GAlow=38,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.39=minChronAge(GAlow=39,GAhigh=40,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      (ga.41=minChronAge(GAlow=40,GAhigh=41,model=model0,ylab='Est. difference',less=less))
      #dev.off()
  }
  
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
    model0.plot = plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age",cond=list(GA=35),col=cColors[1],rug=FALSE,ylim=my.ylim,lwd=2,se=0,ylab=my.ylab,xlab='Chronological age (days)',hide.label=TRUE,main=my.main,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age",cond=list(GA=36),col=cColors[3],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,se=0,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age",  cond=list(GA=37),col=cColors[5],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,se=0,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=38),col=cColors[7],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=39),col=cColors[9],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=40),col=cColors[11],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  plot_smooth(model0, view="chor_age", se=0, cond=list(GA=41),col=cColors[13],add=TRUE,rug=FALSE,lwd=2,print.summary=FALSE,xlim=c(2,201))
  
  if(print.legend==TRUE) {
  legend(x='bottomright',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1)
  }
  if (estMaxChronAge | estMinChronAge) {
    abline(v=ga.41,col=cColors[13],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.39,col=cColors[9],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.38,col=cColors[7],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.37,col=cColors[5],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.36,col=cColors[3],lwd=2,lty=2)
    abline(v=ga.35,col=cColors[1],lwd=2,lty=2)
  }
  my.plot = recordPlot()
  my.plot
}
tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeFA.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_fa.combat, my.ylab = "FX FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.2020068  0.0021918   92.16   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.0003364  0.0028112   -0.12    0.905    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     2.539  3.038  8.687 5.58e-05 ***
## ti(GA)           1.111  1.145 10.252  0.00111 ** 
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.451  1.702  0.242  0.68961    
## s(Unique_ID)    51.680 76.000  2.223 6.49e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.691   Deviance explained =   83%
## -REML = -369.01  Scale est. = 6.4844e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.675
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.633
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.642
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.589
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.543
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.569
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est + a$sim.CI < 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
# check if there is a difference for entire time range or no difference anywhere:
#model0 = gam(Fx_fa.combat~ti(chor_age,k=9)+ti(GA,k=5)+ti(chor_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+Sex+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
#plot_diff(model0,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(35,40)),sim.ci = TRUE, hide.label=TRUE,xlab='Chronological age (days)')


ci.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Ci_fa.combat, my.ylab = "Ci FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.162786   0.001270  128.17   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.001811   0.001631    1.11    0.271    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     3.770  4.436 158.398 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.388  1.497   8.507 0.00105 ** 
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.826  2.157   4.804 0.00936 ** 
## s(Unique_ID)    48.002 76.000   2.024 6.7e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.906   Deviance explained = 94.7%
## -REML = -431.71  Scale est. = 2.4633e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000.

##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)

##      Critical value: 2.712

##  
## Summary:

##

ccg.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCg_fa.combat, my.ylab = "CCg FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.180434   0.001575 114.578   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001119   0.002021  -0.554    0.581    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     2.911  3.483 115.443  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.688  1.893  13.048 1.46e-05 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.449  1.749   6.059  0.01015 *  
## s(Unique_ID)    38.070 76.000   1.092  0.00026 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.842   Deviance explained = 89.8%
## -REML = -397.86  Scale est. = 5.3336e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.695
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.691
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.7
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.667
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.666
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##

##      Critical value: 2.656
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

ccb.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCb_fa.combat, my.ylab="CCb FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.214952   0.001866 115.174   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001823   0.002394  -0.761    0.449    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     3.587  4.232 156.764  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.001  1.001  33.567 4.14e-07 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.085  2.438   5.676  0.00441 ** 
## s(Unique_ID)    54.205 76.000   2.772  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.915   Deviance explained = 95.6%
## -REML = -390.41  Scale est. = 4.1055e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.75
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.682
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.762
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000.

##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)

##      Critical value: 2.741
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

ccs.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCs_fa.combat, my.ylab="CCs FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.169022   0.001920  88.032   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001727   0.002462  -0.701    0.485    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     3.028  3.608 69.743  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.001  1.001  5.386    0.023 *  
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.001  1.001  2.764    0.101    
## s(Unique_ID)    47.502 76.000  1.844 2.82e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.805   Deviance explained = 88.7%
## -REML = -380.92  Scale est. = 5.9099e-05  n = 129

##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.445
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

ilf.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$ILF_fa.combat, my.ylab="ILF FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.1498942  0.0017194  87.178   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.0002159  0.0022048  -0.098    0.922    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

##      Critical value: 2.729
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.698
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
atr.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$ATR_fa.combat, my.ylab="ATR FA")
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.927e-01  1.517e-03 126.990   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -9.987e-05  1.944e-03  -0.051    0.959    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     3.529  4.177 78.825  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000 21.185  1.6e-05 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.395  2.806  4.153 0.008760 ** 
## s(Unique_ID)    41.527 76.000  1.254 0.000146 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.834   Deviance explained = 89.8%
## -REML = -403.85  Scale est. = 4.4447e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.81
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##

##      Critical value: 2.787
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##

##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.)

pt.fa = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_fa.combat, my.ylab="PT FA",print.legend = FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.262586   0.001949 134.757   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        -0.001775   0.002508  -0.708    0.482    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     3.380  3.982 115.756  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.002  1.002  15.253 0.000214 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  5.183  6.888   3.054 0.007644 ** 
## s(Unique_ID)    48.185 76.000   1.955 1.42e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =   0.88   Deviance explained = 93.5%
## -REML = -378.1  Scale est. = 5.6064e-05  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.821
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.876
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##

##      Critical value: 2.908
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##

##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.888
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##  
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##

##      Critical value: 2.907
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)

## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)

##      Critical value: 2.773
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

Check ATR where the significant differences all appear to end at the same time:

model.atr.fa = gam(ATR_fa.combat~ti(chor_age,k=9)+ti(GA,k=5)+ti(chor_age,GA,k=c(9,5))+Sex+s(Unique_ID,bs='re'),method='REML',data=dat_harmonized)
plot_diff(model.atr.fa,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(35,40)),sim.ci = TRUE)
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.783
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## chor_age window(s) of significant difference(s):
##  18.683417 - 88.150754
plot_diff(model.atr.fa,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(36,40)),sim.ci = TRUE)
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.819
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## chor_age window(s) of significant difference(s):
##  18.683417 - 88.150754
plot_diff(model.atr.fa,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(37,40)),sim.ci = TRUE)
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.792
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## chor_age window(s) of significant difference(s):
##  18.683417 - 88.150754
plot_diff(model.atr.fa,view='chor_age',comp=list(GA=c(38,40)),sim.ci = TRUE)
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.801
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 

## 
## chor_age window(s) of significant difference(s):
##  18.683417 - 88.150754

The plots correctly reflect the maximum age range at which a significant difference occurred.

MD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeMD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_md.combat, my.ylab = "FX MD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),estMinChronAge = FALSE,estMaxChronAge=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.29743    0.01904  68.144   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.04524    0.02442   1.853   0.0683 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                   edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.00      1 5.820  0.0186 *  
## ti(GA)           1.00      1 6.022  0.0167 *  
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.00      1 1.310  0.2565    
## s(Unique_ID)    57.28     76 3.042  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.702   Deviance explained = 84.5%
## -REML = -108.59  Scale est. = 0.003805  n = 129
ci = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Ci_md.combat, my.ylab = "Ci MD",my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),less=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age,

##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value

## s(Unique_ID)    55.176 76.000   3.082  < 2e-16 ***

##      Critical value: 2.765

ccg = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCg_md.combat, my.ylab = "CCg MD",my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),less=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.18408    0.01356  87.304   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01039    0.01741   0.597    0.552    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.320  1.530 60.973  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.242  2.496  8.524 0.000636 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.286  1.491  0.608 0.620489    
## s(Unique_ID)    36.874 76.000  0.981 0.000819 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.642   Deviance explained = 76.1%
## -REML = -131.1  Scale est. = 0.0041032  n = 129

##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)

##      Critical value: 2.701
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##

##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
## Warning in max(a$chor_age[a$est - a$sim.CI > 0]): no non-missing arguments to
## max; returning -Inf
ccb = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$CCb_md.combat, my.ylab="CCb MD",my.ylim = c(0.9,1.5),less=FALSE)

##      Critical value: 2.725
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
## 
atr = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$ATR_md.combat, my.ylab="ATR MD",less=FALSE,my.ylim=c(0.9,1.5))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:

## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)

## SexM        0.013590   0.011144   1.219    0.227

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.04331    0.01144  91.211   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01821    0.01473   1.236     0.22    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.848  2.221 29.068 < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.490  2.696  5.677 0.00123 ** 
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.275  2.689  0.905 0.31280    
## s(Unique_ID)    43.834 76.000  1.476 2.5e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.658   Deviance explained = 79.5%
## -REML = -157.1  Scale est. = 0.0022795  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

AD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeAD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)

par(mfrow=c(4,3))

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 1.178107   0.009763 120.672  < 2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.036168   0.012540   2.884  0.00524 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     4.047  4.742 69.470  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           1.000  1.000 17.394 8.70e-05 ***

## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.34198    0.01178 113.896   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01964    0.01517   1.295    0.199    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.001  1.003 38.557  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.518  2.736  4.699  0.00325 ** 
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.134  2.541  0.714  0.39545    
## s(Unique_ID)    42.123 76.000  1.316 8.42e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.597   Deviance explained = 75.1%
## -REML = -152.42  Scale est. = 0.0025853  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2

AF is strange: small region over which there are significant differences, approximately 50-70 days. For clarify, don’t plus the significance lines.

RD

tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeRD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)
par(mfrow=c(4,3))

fx = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_rd.combat, my.ylab = "FX RD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),estMinChronAge = FALSE,estMaxChronAge=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.15422    0.01893  60.976   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.04226    0.02428   1.741   0.0864 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                   edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.00      1 6.688  0.0119 *  
## ti(GA)           1.00      1 6.422  0.0137 *  
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.00      1 1.227  0.2720    
## s(Unique_ID)    57.56     76 3.113  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.709   Deviance explained = 84.9%
## -REML = -109.69  Scale est. = 0.0037033  n = 129
ci = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Ci_rd.combat, my.ylab = "Ci RD",my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),less=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.991560   0.005224 189.809   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.015700   0.006742   2.329   0.0231 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df       F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     5.285  6.028 271.270  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.292  2.424  22.372 1.71e-07 ***

## s(Unique_ID)    54.610 76.000   3.012  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.962   Deviance explained = 98.2%
## -REML = -261.65  Scale est. = 0.00028602  n = 129
## Summary:
##  * Sex : factor; set to the value(s): M. 
##  * chor_age : numeric predictor; with 200 values ranging from 10.000000 to 202.000000. 
##  * Unique_ID : factor; set to the value(s): 00106_04. (Might be canceled as random effect, check below.) 
##  * NOTE : The following random effects columns are canceled: s(Unique_ID)
##  
##  * Simultaneous 95%-CI used : 
##      Critical value: 2.802
##      Proportion posterior simulations in pointwise CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##      Proportion posterior simulations in simultaneous CI: 1 (10000 samples)
##

## Family: gaussian

##

pt = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_rd.combat, my.ylab="PT RD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinChronAge=FALSE,estMaxChronAge=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.89400    0.01141  78.359   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01747    0.01470   1.189    0.238    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     2.078  2.494 31.772  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.465  2.666  6.124 0.000832 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.323  2.738  1.045 0.265700    
## s(Unique_ID)    44.696 76.000  1.566 1.43e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.685   Deviance explained = 81.4%
## -REML = -158.01  Scale est. = 0.0021939  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2
tiff('../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeRD.tiff',width=1080,height=1280,pointsize=18)
par(mfrow=c(4,3))

#pdf(file='../Figures/TractSpecific_GestChronAgeRD.pdf')

fx = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Fx_rd.combat, my.ylab = "FX RD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),estMinChronAge = FALSE,estMaxChronAge=FALSE)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.15422    0.01893  60.976   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.04226    0.02428   1.741   0.0864 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                   edf Ref.df     F p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     1.00      1 6.688  0.0119 *  
## ti(GA)           1.00      1 6.422  0.0137 *  
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.00      1 1.227  0.2720    
## s(Unique_ID)    57.56     76 3.113  <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.709   Deviance explained = 84.9%
## -REML = -109.69  Scale est. = 0.0037033  n = 129
ci = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$Ci_rd.combat, my.ylab = "Ci RD",my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4),less=FALSE)

ifof = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$IFOF_rd.combat, my.ylab="IFOF RD",less=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:

## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.925084   0.009435  98.051   <2e-16 ***
## SexM        0.026328   0.012173   2.163   0.0338 *

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

## ti(GA)           1.511  1.652 10.570 0.000196 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  1.077  1.136  4.626 0.035151 *

## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

##      Critical value: 2.647

pt = my.function.ga(dti.tract = dat_harmonized$PT_rd.combat, my.ylab="PT RD",print.legend = FALSE,less=FALSE,estMinChronAge=FALSE,estMaxChronAge=FALSE,my.ylim = c(0.8,1.4))
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## dti.tract ~ ti(chor_age, k = 9) + ti(GA, k = 5) + ti(chor_age, 
##     GA, k = c(9, 5)) + Sex + s(Unique_ID, bs = "re")
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.89400    0.01141  78.359   <2e-16 ***
## SexM         0.01747    0.01470   1.189    0.238    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                    edf Ref.df      F  p-value    
## ti(chor_age)     2.078  2.494 31.772  < 2e-16 ***
## ti(GA)           2.465  2.666  6.124 0.000832 ***
## ti(chor_age,GA)  2.323  2.738  1.045 0.265700    
## s(Unique_ID)    44.696 76.000  1.566 1.43e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.685   Deviance explained = 81.4%
## -REML = -158.01  Scale est. = 0.0021939  n = 129
plot.new()
  cColors = viridis::plasma(15)
  
legend(x='topleft',legend=c('GA=35','GA=36','GA=37','GA=38','GA=39','GA=40','GA=41'),lty=1,lwd=2,col=cColors[seq(1,13,by=2)],bty='n',cex=1.2)

dev.off()
## png 
##   2
